project_title: Assessing the Impact of Zoning Policy Changes on Urban Compactness via Causal Inference and Satellite Time-Series

topic_screening.brief_rationale: Employs synthetic control methods on longitudinal land-cover data to evaluate the effectiveness of urban density policies in metropolitan corridors.
topic_screening.publishability: Computers, Environment and Urban Systems; Landscape and Urban Planning; Journal of the American Planning Association

research_questions: How do specific zoning policy changes, such as urban growth boundaries and transit-oriented development upzoning, affect urban compactness in metropolitan statistical areas?
To what extent do these zoning policies reduce spatial fragmentation and increase built-up area density over a 20-year period?
hypotheses: Implementation of urban growth boundaries and upzoning policies significantly increases the urban compactness index compared to a synthetic counterfactual.
Zoning policy changes lead to a measurable decrease in the spatial fragmentation index and an increase in built-up area density in treated MSAs.
unit_of_analysis: Metropolitan statistical areas (MSAs) and 1km x 1km grid cells over a 20-year annual panel.

exposures.name: Zoning policy changes
exposures.definition: Binary treatment indicator for the implementation year of specific zoning policy changes (e.g., urban growth boundaries, transit-oriented development upzoning)

data_sources.name: National Land Cover Database (NLCD) / ESA WorldCover
data_sources.accessibility: Freely available via public API and bulk download without paywalls, providing longitudinal satellite-derived land cover classifications.

data_sources.name: National Zoning and Land Use Database
data_sources.accessibility: Publicly accessible repository of municipal zoning records and enactment dates, freely available for academic use.

data_sources.name: US Census Bureau API
data_sources.accessibility: Free, public API providing demographic and economic covariates at the MSA and tract levels without manual approval.

methodology.estimand_and_strategy: Average Treatment Effect on the Treated (ATT). The strategy relies on the Synthetic Control Method (SCM) to construct a counterfactual for treated cities using a donor pool of untreated cities, assuming parallel trajectories in the pre-treatment period conditional on matched covariates.
methodology.model_family: Synthetic Control Method (SCM) and Generalized Synthetic Control
robustness_checks: In-space placebo tests assigning treatment to untreated units in the donor pool
In-time placebo tests shifting the treatment year backwards to check for anticipation effects
Leave-one-out sensitivity analysis iteratively dropping cities from the donor pool
Alternative compactness metrics such as Moran's I versus simple density
Varying the pre-treatment matching window length
Controlling for time-varying covariates like local GDP and population growth
Alternative estimators including Staggered Difference-in-Differences (Callaway & Sant'Anna)
expected_tables_figures: Treatment vs. Synthetic Control trend lines pre and post-treatment
Gap plot showing the difference between treated and synthetic control over time
Placebo distribution plot with permutation test p-values
Table of pre-treatment covariate balance between treated and synthetic units
Map of the study area showing treated units and donor pool weights
